title: PID 1, Signals, Shutdown, Health, Restart, and Resource Constraints · Delivery Case: Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits
summary: A queue worker ignores termination, accumulates zombies, reports healthy while stalled, and can exhaust host memory. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker cpu memory pids limits increment in bounded process lifecycle and health policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.
Choose no, on-failure, always, or unless-stopped behavior while accounting for manual stops, daemon restarts, and dependency recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 384 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior. A defensible solution applies Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded process lifecycle and health policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-cpu-memory-pids-limits — Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-process-signals-health-resources-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-process-signals-health-resources-5
    artifact: runtime-policy.yaml
    decision: worked docker process signals health resources 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      init: true
      stop_grace_period: 30s
      healthcheck: bounded-readiness-probe
      restart: on-failure:3
      memory: 256m
      pids_limit: 128
    verify: docker inspect training-worker --format json && docker events --since 10m
    failure: Reject an unbounded probe, restart loop as recovery, shell wrapper that swallows signals, or deployment without memory and PID limits.
    repair: Make PID 1 ownership explicit, bound shutdown and health timing, preserve durable work, and test termination plus resource pressure.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Containers receive safe CPU and memory limits by default. This fails the Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
